caveats: a: Occupation evidence is inferred from broader chemical manufacturing.
a: Product and plant automation vary.
rho: No industry-specific five-year adoption survey.
rho: Environmental and performance accountability requires expert review.
e: Injected firm count is a margin-bridged estimate.
e: Industry classification alone does not prove independence or transferability.
s5: Broad demographic proxy.
s5: No public transfer-rate denominator was found.
q: No public contract evidence quantifies pass-through.
q: Inkjet and specialty packaging inks may retain more than conventional publication inks.
d5: Official sources define and track manufacturing but do not supply a clean five-year product-volume forecast.
d5: Mix differs sharply by target.
o: Demand elimination is captured primarily in d5.
o: Operator share can migrate to integrated equipment vendors or overseas suppliers.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat B2B sales of color- and process-qualified consumables, commonly accompanied by matching, formulation, troubleshooting, inventory, and regulatory support.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive ink production, pure distributors, printer operations without ink manufacture, shells, and firms outside the EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: Independent LMM manufacturers of printing and inkjet inks and cartridges with repeat external sales to printers, converters, packagers, or equipment users, including formulation and technical support.

a.bridge: BLS and EPA materials identify this as chemical manufacturing with physical ink processes and environmental-control needs, but no six-digit wage-weighted task study was found. I translate the broader chemical-manufacturing workflow: color/formulation search, specifications, SDS and compliance documents, scheduling, purchasing, quoting, technical-service triage, and quality-data analysis are exposed; mixing, milling, filling, lab execution, maintenance, and material handling are much less so. This may overstate exposure in small production-heavy plants.
caveats: Occupation evidence is inferred from broader chemical manufacturing.
Product and plant automation vary.
a.change_evidence: Payroll-weighted task mapping and current-software inventory across 8-12 independent ink plants, separated by conventional, packaging, and inkjet products, would materially revise the range.
a.rationale: AI can assist technical and administrative work, but much employment is tied to accountable physical formulation, production, quality, and field service.

caveats: Official sources define and track manufacturing but do not supply a clean five-year product-volume forecast.
Mix differs sharply by target.
d5.change_evidence: Constant-price shipment and volume forecasts split by publication, commercial, packaging, label, textile, industrial, and inkjet chemistries, reconciled to target customer mix, would materially move the range.
d5.rationale: Digitization reduces publication and office-print demand, while packaging, labels, industrial applications, and inkjet support resilience. The combined current basket is therefore expected to contract modestly in real quantity, with a plausible growth case if packaging and digital formats dominate.

caveats: Injected firm count is a margin-bridged estimate.
Industry classification alone does not prove independence or transferability.
e.change_evidence: A firm census using business databases, EPA facility records, product catalogs, and interviews for all 53 injected candidates would establish operating model and eligibility.
e.rationale: Independent LMM ink makers generally have repeat external consumables revenue, but captive units, cartridge-only assemblers, distributor-heavy companies, environmentally impaired sites, obsolete product exposure, and customer concentration reduce eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 325910 ($592,983,024) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 325910 ($4,251,277,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.1801 (margins.json[325]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Chemical (Specialty) EBITDA/Sales 18.01%); revenue/employee $465,180 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 325910); class <5: 45 firms, avg revenue/firm $708,600 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $127,619 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 29 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,759,966 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $497,070 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 21 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,388,048 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $610,187 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 36 firms, avg revenue/firm $14,632,389 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,635,293 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 17 firms, avg revenue/firm $51,180,529 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $9,217,613 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 15 firms, avg revenue/firm $178,091,133 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $32,074,213 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 53

caveats: Demand elimination is captured primarily in d5.
Operator share can migrate to integrated equipment vendors or overseas suppliers.
o.change_evidence: Printer and converter surveys on digital substitution, in-house ink production, cartridge-system shifts, and supplier consolidation would move the operator-required share.
o.rationale: Where printing remains, a physical ink or cartridge must be manufactured and performance-qualified. Software can eliminate some printed output or automate ordering and formulation work, but cannot replace the material consumed in retained applications.

caveats: No public contract evidence quantifies pass-through.
Inkjet and specialty packaging inks may retain more than conventional publication inks.
q.change_evidence: Customer contracts, price-cost lag data, formula ownership, renewal histories, and win/loss interviews at targets would directly test retention.
q.rationale: Qualified color and press performance, technical support, and switching costs protect some savings. Large printers and packaging converters can negotiate price, raw-material mechanisms are common, and commodity formulations face price competition, causing benefit sharing over renewals.

caveats: No industry-specific five-year adoption survey.
Environmental and performance accountability requires expert review.
rho.change_evidence: Controlled pilots measuring labor hours, off-spec rates, formulation iterations, and customer response time across representative manufacturers would move the estimate.
rho.rationale: Document, formulation-search, quality-trend, scheduling, and customer-support aids can be implemented in five years, while proprietary sparse data, color-performance validation, legacy ERP/LIMS integration, safety review, and customer press trials slow realization.

s5.bridge: Federal Reserve age-of-owner chartbooks cover small firms economy-wide, not printing-ink control transfers. I treat older ownership as succession pressure and discount for internal succession, closures, minority deals, and non-transferable environmental or customer risks. Consolidation could raise strategic transfers, while secular print decline can instead produce closures.
caveats: Broad demographic proxy.
No public transfer-rate denominator was found.
s5.change_evidence: A denominator-based review of PitchBook/CapIQ transactions, closures, and ownership changes among U.S. ink manufacturers since 2018, plus broker and owner interviews, would change this range.
s5.rationale: A mature niche and consolidation logic create deals, but a small universe, technical customer ties, site liabilities, and declining legacy-print exposures constrain qualifying transfers.

principal_driver: Repeat specification-qualified consumable sales create switching friction and recurring demand in retained print applications.
principal_weakness: Legacy print decline and physical production constrain both demand and labor substitution.

sources.supports: Census defines the industry as manufacturing printing and inkjet inks and inkjet cartridges.
sources.title: NAICS 325910 Printing Ink Manufacturing

sources.supports: EPA provides printing-ink manufacturing process and emission-factor material, supporting the physical-process and environmental-accountability characterization.

sources.supports: EPA identifies printing-sector air-toxics regulation and links process information that includes printing ink.
sources.title: Printing and Related Support Activities Sector

sources.supports: Federal Reserve chartbooks include owner-demographic and age-of-owner evidence used only as a succession proxy.
sources.title: 2024 Firms in Focus chartbooks on small business data

sources.supports: Census provides historical manufacturing shipments and orders resources, supporting the proposed route for demand verification rather than a specific forecast.

title: Printing Ink Manufacturing
